# t-linter

> Intelligent syntax highlighting and validation for Python template strings (PEP 750)

- [t-linter](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/): Intelligent syntax highlighting, validation, and formatting for Python template strings ([PEP 750](https://peps.python.org/pep-0750/)).
- [Installation](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/installation/): t-linter can be installed in three ways depending on your use case.
## Usage

- [VSCode Extension](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/vscode/): VSCode supports three t-linter save-time formatting modes: Ruff coexistence mode, composed Ruff + t-linter formatter mode, and t-linter formatter m...
- [Interpolation Type Checking](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/interpolation-type-checking/): Interpolation type checking is an opt-in LSP feature that checks values inserted into typed JSON, YAML, and TOML template strings. It uses each tem...
### CLI

- [Check Command](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/cli/check/): The `check` command validates Python template strings for syntax errors in embedded languages.
- [Format Command](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/cli/format/): The `format` command rewrites supported Python template strings in place.
- [LSP Server](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/cli/lsp/): t-linter includes a built-in Language Server Protocol (LSP) server for editor integration.

- [Configuration](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/usage/configuration/): t-linter can be configured via `pyproject.toml` and ignore files.

- [Supported Languages](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/supported-languages/): t-linter supports syntax highlighting and validation for the following embedded languages in Python template strings.
## Optional

- [Roadmap](https://t-linter.koxudaxi.dev/roadmap/)
